frameworks.name: proactive sensory adaptation
frameworks.one_line: adaptation prospectively retunes encoding for the next expected input

frameworks.name: information theoretic encoding analysis
frameworks.one_line: quantifies how coding resources are reallocated across orientation

frameworks.name: natural scene statistics
frameworks.one_line: temporal statistics of retinal orientations predict encoding shift

core_question: Does sensory adaptation proactively optimize encoding for the next expected stimulus rather than merely reacting to past input?
key_assumptions: total coding capacity is conserved across adaptation states
discrimination thresholds index local encoding accuracy
retinal input statistics reflect ecologically relevant priors
manipulations: comparison of post-adaptation discrimination at multiple test orientations
analysis of natural retinal input from freely behaving subjects
key_findings: discrimination accuracy peaks at the adaptor orientation
total coding capacity remains constant across adaptation
local orientation distribution in retinal input peaks at recent mean orientation
interpretations: adaptation reallocates coding resources to optimize for the next expected input
encoding changes are matched to short-history natural input statistics
